current_fact.fact_text: In 1721, Johann Sebastian Bach dedicated six concertos to Margrave Christian Ludwig of Brandenburg-Schwedt, works that would become known as the Brandenburg Concertos. These pieces, now considered masterpieces of Baroque music, were largely overlooked in his lifetime and only gained true fame centuries later.

episode.title: Morning Drops #01 - Timeless Legacy
episode.description: Gaia guides us through timeless music to start the morning with the right foot! The first episode of the Morning Drops series — classic sounds that never fade.
songs: Roberta Flack - Killing me softly with his song

current_fact.match_reason: The Brandenburg Concertos are a quintessential 'timeless legacy' in music history, fitting an episode devoted to classic sounds that never fade.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 1992, Space Shuttle Atlantis launched on mission STS-45, carrying the ATLAS-1 science platform. The crew conducted pioneering studies of Earth's atmosphere, helping scientists better understand ozone, climate, and how our planet interacts with the Sun.

episode.title: Morning Drops #40 - Diving With Orcas
episode.description: Yasmin's friend Caroline takes us beyond the polar circle, into the Arctic waters of Norway, where orcas and humpback whales gather each winter. A personal journey shaped by a quiet obsession with orcas, from Patagonia to the icy fjords of the north. The soundscape moves gently between cinematic swells and soft, rhythmic pulses — echoing the cold light of Arctic mornings and the grace of underwater encounters.

queue.match_reason: STS-45 studied Earth’s atmosphere from orbit, while the episode follows a journey through Earth's polar atmosphere and oceans; both are quiet explorations of our fragile, blue planet from different vantage points.

queue.fact_text: In 1882, physician Robert Koch announced his discovery of Mycobacterium tuberculosis, the bacterium that causes tuberculosis. His work not only transformed our understanding of infectious disease, but also helped establish the very foundations of microbiology and modern germ theory.

episode.title: Morning Drops #42 - The Joy Of Learning
episode.description:
Hosted by Yasmin, this Morning Drop explores learning as a lifelong process, focusing on persistence, repetition, and the emotional landscape that comes with challenging oneself over time.

Through personal stories and scientific insights, the episode looks at how learning reshapes the brain, generates joy through effort, and why progress often arrives after confusion and frustration. It reflects on the so-called delayed pleasure effect, the role of dopamine in motivation, and how curiosity and learning support long-term wellbeing.

The episode also questions our cultural relationship with failure and invites a softer, more realistic approach to learning, one that accepts mistakes as part of growth and sees persistence as a quiet form of strength.

Mood: reflective, grounded, quietly hopeful
Music genre: alternative rock, hip hop, electronic, neo-soul, drill

A space to listen slowly and stay with the process.

queue.match_reason: Koch’s painstaking discovery of the TB bacterium is a landmark in scientific learning, mirroring the episode’s focus on persistence, curiosity, and how hard-won understanding reshapes our world.

queue.fact_text: On the night of March 24, 1944, seventy-six Allied prisoners of war escaped from the German camp Stalag Luft III through a hand-dug tunnel, an event later dramatized in the film "The Great Escape." Though most were recaptured, the operation remains one of the most audacious and meticulously planned prison breaks in history.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

queue.match_reason: The Great Escape was a daring act of resistance against a brutal regime; pairing it with an episode about Rage Against the Machine amplifies the shared themes of defiance, strategy, and refusal to submit.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 1976, Argentina’s armed forces overthrew President Isabel Perón in a military coup, beginning a seven-year dictatorship known as the National Reorganization Process. The period became infamous for its "Dirty War," during which thousands of people were disappeared in a climate of fear and censorship.

episode.title: Morning Drops #44 - Residente
episode.description:
In this edition of Morning Drops, Yasmin navigates the complex geography of René Pérez Joglar, known to the world as Residente. His story is not just a musical evolution; it is a timeline marked by resistance, lyrical obsession, and a relentless search for identity.

From the gated streets of Trujillo Alto to the global stage, we explore how a restless mind transformed into the voice of a generation. The selection moves through the textures of his life: hearing the raw energy of his early rebellion, the sharp precision of his political critique, and finally, the fragile silence of his most personal confessions.

It is a study in duality. We witness the rapper who dismantles giants and the son who misses his mother. It is a soundscape that proves that sometimes, the loudest revolution happens inside.

Mood: Raw, Revolutionary, Introspective, Honest
Music genre: Latin Alternative, Hip Hop, Urban, World

queue.match_reason: The Argentine coup and ensuing dictatorship resonate with Residente’s lifelong engagement with Latin American memory, state violence, and political critique.

queue.fact_text: In the Nanking Incident of March 24, 1927, foreign warships bombarded Nanjing as anti-foreigner riots erupted in the city. The chaos unfolded amid China’s turbulent struggle between rival political forces, exposing the fraught intersection of nationalism, imperialism, and civilian life.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

queue.match_reason: The incident centers on foreigners in a city in turmoil; this reflective travel-themed episode encourages more mindful cross-cultural presence, a counterpoint to a history of violent encounters between locals and outsiders.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 1980, Salvadoran Archbishop Óscar Romero was assassinated while celebrating Mass in San Salvador. Known for his outspoken defense of the poor and criticism of human rights abuses, his killing turned him into a global symbol of faith, justice, and martyrdom.

episode.title: Morning Drops #34 - Soul Sisters
episode.description: A tribute to four iconic voices — women who have shaped the way we listen, feel, and move. Each track carries a different kind of presence: one that holds you, one that questions, one that grounds, and one that lets go. An energy that is soulful, raw, and unapologetically feminine.
songs: Grace Jones - Walking in the Rain
Aretha Franklin - A Rose Is Still a Rose

queue.match_reason: Romero’s life was rooted in moral courage and giving voice to the voiceless; an episode honoring powerful, truth-telling voices in music channels a similar spirit of speaking out with soul and conviction.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 1977, Morarji Desai became prime minister of India, the first person to hold the office who did not belong to the Indian National Congress. His election marked a dramatic shift after the Emergency period and showed that India’s massive democracy could unseat even its longest-dominant party.

episode.title: Morning Drops #20 - Hitchhiking Poland
episode.description: Yasmin takes you back to the summer of 2020, when borders were still uncertain and a long-held wish to visit Poland finally came true. Through four tracks, tracing her journey from a stranger's couch in Poznań to the peaks of Zakopane.

queue.match_reason: Desai’s rise signaled political change through the will of ordinary citizens; a hitchhiking journey through Poland likewise moves through everyday people, landscapes and choices that quietly reshape political maps of the world.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 1946, a British Cabinet Mission arrived in India to negotiate the transfer of power from the British Raj to Indian leaders. Though its plan ultimately failed, the mission’s debates and proposals laid much of the groundwork for the eventual partition into India and Pakistan.

episode.title: Morning Drops #22 - Sicilians On The Dolomites
episode.description: Hosted by Yasmin, a musical journey inspired by the mountains. Joined by two special guests, exploring the tracks they've chosen, hearing the stories behind their picks, and discovering the personal memories each song brings to life — a blend of music, voices, and landscapes you can hear.
songs: Florian Pellissier Quintet - Coup de foudre à Thessalonique
Lucio Dalla & Francesco De Gregori - Ma come fanno i marinai

queue.match_reason: The Cabinet Mission was essentially a journey of delegates into a vast, complex landscape to negotiate the future; this episode’s mountain journey with multiple voices mirrors a meeting of perspectives in a charged landscape.

queue.fact_text: In Kraków on March 24, 1794, Tadeusz Kościuszko proclaimed a national uprising against Imperial Russia and the Kingdom of Prussia, assuming command of Polish forces. His call ignited the Kościuszko Uprising, a last-ditch attempt to defend the fading Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth.

episode.title: Morning Drops #24 - The Rise Of Afrobeat
episode.description: An exploration of the rise of Afrobeat, tracing its roots and evolution as a genre that blends African rhythms with jazz, funk, and highlife. A celebration of the infectious energy and cultural significance of this influential musical movement.

queue.match_reason: Kościuszko’s proclamation was a freedom struggle rooted in identity and resistance; an episode chronicling the rise of Afrobeat, a music born from political consciousness and liberation, parallels that fight in a different region and century.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 2018, students across the United States organized the March for Our Lives, demanding stronger gun control after the Stoneman Douglas High School shooting. It became one of the largest youth-led protests in American history, showcasing a new generation mobilizing through social media and street action.

episode.title: Morning Drops #04 - Bars With Balls
episode.description: Yasmin dives into Women's Day through the voices of four incredible female rappers. Morning fire from the magic planet of Sedna FM — move your hands up and down with us. Hip hop culture through a feminine lens.
songs: Little Simz - Point and Kill (feat. Obongjayar)

queue.match_reason: A massive, outspoken youth protest aligns with an episode centered on powerful, politically sharp rap voices taking space and speaking openly about change and justice.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 1999, NATO began airstrikes against Yugoslavia during the Kosovo War, the first time the alliance attacked a sovereign state without UN Security Council approval. The controversial intervention reshaped debates about humanitarian war and the limits of national sovereignty.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - A Trip-Hop Journey
episode.description: Hosted by friend Lorenzo, a journey through the roots and evolution of trip-hop. From Bristol's early experiments with Massive Attack and Tricky, to DJ Shadow's cinematic sampling in California, and Thievery Corporation's global downtempo grooves in Washington D.C. — exploring how this genre blurred boundaries and reshaped electronic music.
songs: Tricky - Hell Is Round the Corner
DJ Shadow - Midnight in a Perfect World

queue.match_reason: A boundary-crossing military campaign sits alongside a boundary-blurring musical genre; the cinematic, uneasy tones of trip-hop mirror the moral ambiguity and global tension of the Kosovo intervention era.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 1998, Dr. Rüdiger Marmulla performed the first computer-assisted Bone Segment Navigation surgery in Regensburg, Germany. By combining imaging and real-time tracking, his work opened a new era in precise, minimally invasive cranio-maxillofacial procedures.

episode.title: Morning Drops #47 - The Rise Of Autotune
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin explores the explosive rise of Auto-Tune, a tool that quietly changed the sound of music without asking for attention. From fragile, imperfect recordings to voices shaped with precision, this episode follows the moment when correction became standard and sound became controlled.

Along the way, we discover the curious story of how it was invented, how it moved from the background of studios to the center of debate, and how a few artists turned it into something expressive rather than hidden.

Listen slowly, and let us know what you think about Autotune.
songs: Chet Baker – I Fall in Love Too Easily

queue.match_reason: Computer-guided surgery and Auto-Tune both show technology stepping in to correct and refine human work with uncanny precision, raising similar questions about where human ends and machine begins.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 1982, Bangladeshi President Abdus Sattar was ousted in a bloodless coup led by Army Chief Hussain Muhammad Ershad, who suspended the constitution and imposed martial law. The coup ushered in nearly a decade of military-dominated politics in the young nation.

episode.title: Morning Drops #27 - Nomophobia
episode.description: Yasmin reflects on what happened when she recently lost her phone and why it felt freeing. A dive into the psychology of our relationship with smartphones: from nomophobia (the fear of being without a phone), to digital burnout and cognitive overload, to the surprising benefits of boredom and the brain's default mode network. An invitation to rethink how we connect and disconnect.

queue.match_reason: A sudden military takeover rewrites the rules of a whole society overnight; the episode’s reflection on how invisible systems (like phones) control our behavior invites a quieter, personal look at power and control in daily life.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 1939, about forty members of a pro-Nazi group attempted the Liechtenstein putsch, marching from the village of Nendeln toward the capital, Vaduz, hoping to trigger annexation by Germany. The coup fizzled, and Liechtenstein remained one of Europe’s smallest but most resilient monarchies.

episode.title: Morning Drops #17 - Steel City
episode.description: Gaia takes us on a short trip through Sheffield's sounds — from electronic textures to raw guitars and sharp storytelling. Four tracks, one city, different edges of the same story.
songs: The Human League - The Sound Of The Crowd

queue.match_reason: A tiny country facing attempted ideological takeover contrasts with an episode that maps one city’s stubbornly distinct sound; both tell stories about local identity resisting larger political and cultural tides.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 1387, an English fleet defeated a combined Franco-Castilian-Flemish armada in the Battle of Margate off the coast of Kent. The battle, fought largely as a running chase at sea, temporarily eased French pressure on English shipping during the Hundred Years’ War.

episode.title: Morning Drops #31 - Sailing With Germans
episode.description: From the deck of a sailing boat, hosted by Yasmin and her special guests. A week at sea with six Germans, each with their own stories, songs, and ways of moving through the waves. From shared meals to unexpected laughter, from quiet resilience to sun-soaked joy — a collection of voices and tracks that reflect what it means to journey together.
songs: Ben Howard - Keep Your Head Up

queue.match_reason: A medieval naval battle is an intense historical echo of life at sea; this gentle sailing episode provides a present-day, peaceful counter-image of humans and ships moving across the water.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 2023, a violent EF4 tornado ripped through Rolling Fork and Silver City, Mississippi, leveling neighborhoods and leaving a trail of destruction. The storm was so powerful that radar detected debris thrown thousands of feet into the air.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

queue.match_reason: A devastating storm tearing through communities is balanced with an episode about rain, resilience, and the hope that bad weather—literal and emotional—doesn’t last forever.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 2018, Turkish Armed Forces and allied Syrian rebel groups announced full control of Syria’s Afrin District, marking the end of the Afrin offensive. The campaign displaced tens of thousands of civilians and reshaped the map of control in northern Syria.

episode.title: Morning Drops #16 - The Sound of Tuareg
episode.description: A journey into the sound of the Tuareg: from traditional chants and desert rituals to the electrified rhythms of exile and resistance. Music shaped by sand, memory, and survival.

queue.match_reason: The struggle over Afrin sits within broader conflicts about land, borders, and displaced peoples; Tuareg desert blues is likewise born of exile, contested homelands, and the sound of survival in arid frontiers.

queue.fact_text: On March 24, 2015, Germanwings Flight 9525 was deliberately crashed into the French Alps by its co-pilot, killing all 150 people on board. The tragedy raised urgent questions about mental health in aviation, cockpit security, and how we trust invisible systems when we fly.

episode.title: Morning Drops #43 - Looking Back
episode.description:
In this first Morning Drop of 2026, Yasmin opens a space for quiet reflection on the nature of transitions. The episode traces a personal journey that began three years ago, when a sudden injury on New Year’s Day turned a desire for movement into a mandatory season of stillness.

Through the story of a broken knee and the slow process of relearning to walk, the narrative explores the difference between being strong and being aware. It touches on the vulnerability of the body, the unexpected joy found in daily progress, and the warmth that comes from finally allowing oneself to accept help. The musical selection mirrors this path of recovery, moving from the uncertainty of change to the steady rhythm of healing.

Mood: Reflective, Resilient, Grounded
Music genre: Indie Pop, Cinematic, World, Soul

We invite you to listen deeply and find comfort in your own pace.
songs: Kelly Finnigan - I Don't Wanna Wait
Goran Bregović & Iggy Pop - This Is a Film

queue.match_reason: A catastrophic, intentional crash calls for a quiet, respectful pairing; this reflective episode on vulnerability, healing, and looking back creates space to honour trauma while gently moving toward recovery.

queue.fact_text: The discovery of Comet Shoemaker–Levy 9 on March 24, 1993, led to an unprecedented prediction: astronomers could forecast the exact week a comet would smash into a planet. When its fragments hit Jupiter in July 1994, the impact scars were larger than Earth and visible even in small telescopes.

episode.title: Evening Flows #01 - Yasmin - Crescendo in the Medina
episode.description: The first Evening Flow episode comes from Yasmin. It starts deep in the medina, slow, mysterious, textured, and grows into a liberating dance across sub-Saharan landscapes. A journey through ambient soundscapes, Arabic melodies, Afro-Latin rhythms, and funky highlife grooves.
songs: David Visan & Carlos Campos - Crystal Heart
Evan Mars - Ya Zareef Al Tool
Dele Sosimi & Medlar - You No Fit Touch Am (Medlar Remix)

queue.match_reason: The comet’s plunge into Jupiter was a cosmic crescendo, beginning as a faint, mysterious discovery and building into a planetary-scale spectacle—mirroring this mix’s slow ambient start that swells into rhythmic, planetary dance.

queue.fact_text: Bach’s Brandenburg Concertos, dedicated on March 24, 1721, were essentially a musical portfolio he sent to a potential patron, showcasing every trick he could do with an orchestra. Ironically, there is no evidence the Margrave ever had them performed, and the manuscript sat forgotten in his library for over a century.

episode.title: Sedna FM #01 - November Bomb
episode.description: The first Family & Friends radio episode from Yasmin Sarbaoui and Gaia Parolini. Where it all began — a life soundtrack that launched Sedna FM into the universe. Raw, heartfelt, and full of the energy that defines everything that followed.
songs: Canserbero - De la vida como pelicula, tragedia, comedia y ficcion

queue.match_reason: The Brandenburgs were Bach’s big 'first statement' to the world, much like Sedna’s debut episode—a bold, eclectic portfolio meant to show everything this universe of sound can do.

queue.fact_text: Robert Koch’s announcement on March 24, 1882, that he had identified the tuberculosis bacterium gave doctors, for the first time, a clear enemy to target against a disease that then killed one in seven people in Europe. Today, World TB Day is held every year on March 24 in his honor.

episode.title: Morning Drops #19 - Wabi Sabi
episode.description: Wabi-sabi is a story about a handmade mug, a small mistake, and the idea that imperfection can be something to keep—not correct. In this Morning Drop, Gaia shares a moment that didn’t go to plan, and four tracks that echo the same spirit: rough takes, studio accidents, lo-fi textures, and quiet surprises. A soft, thoughtful selection moving between warm electronics, classic riffs, and raw edges.A reminder that not everything needs to be polished. Sometimes, it’s better that way.

queue.match_reason: Koch’s work made invisible microbes visible and showed that our bodies are never perfectly clean or controlled; a wabi-sabi meditation on imperfection and hidden textures resonates with this more nuanced understanding of life, illness, and resilience.

queue.fact_text: The March for Our Lives on March 24, 2018, was remarkable not only for its size, but for how teenagers crafted speeches, artwork, and even protest songs to demand change. It turned grief and anger into a shared cultural moment, blending activism with art and storytelling.

episode.title: Morning Drops #51 - The Dancefloor
episode.description:
For International Women’s Day, this Morning Drop turns its focus to women on the dancefloor, not as a trend, but as a cultural force.

Across five tracks, Gaia follows a line from disco closers to hip hop club classics, tracing how the dancefloor became a place where women could take space, build confidence, and create connection, sometimes with friends, sometimes alone, always with music at the centre.

This episode isn’t about pointing fingers. It’s a gentle reminder that spaces like the dancefloor still matter: places where people can express themselves, meet each other with respect, and feel part of something bigger than a night out.

Tracklist:
Donna Summer - Last Dance (1978)
The Sequence  - Funk you up
Salt-N-Pepa - Push It
Queen Latifah - Ladies First
Alicia Bridges - I Love the Nightlife

queue.match_reason: Both the march and this episode explore how public spaces—streets or dancefloors—can become arenas for collective expression, empowerment, and cultural change, especially led by young voices.

published.fact_text: On March 24, 1993, astronomers Carolyn and Eugene Shoemaker and David Levy discovered Comet Shoemaker–Levy 9 at Palomar Observatory. The comet later broke apart and spectacularly crashed into Jupiter in 1994, giving scientists a rare, real-time look at a major impact event in our solar system.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

published.match_reason: A comet discovery and later collision literally 'shifts' the space around Jupiter; the episode’s theme of reimagining emptiness as creative space echoes how a seemingly distant icy fragment became a cosmic laboratory.

published.fact_text: In 1721, Johann Sebastian Bach dedicated six concertos to Margrave Christian Ludwig of Brandenburg-Schwedt, works that would become known as the Brandenburg Concertos. These pieces, now considered masterpieces of Baroque music, were largely overlooked in his lifetime and only gained true fame centuries later.

episode.title: Morning Drops #01 - Timeless Legacy
episode.description: Gaia guides us through timeless music to start the morning with the right foot! The first episode of the Morning Drops series — classic sounds that never fade.
songs: Roberta Flack - Killing me softly with his song

published.match_reason: The Brandenburg Concertos are a quintessential 'timeless legacy' in music history, fitting an episode devoted to classic sounds that never fade.
